arxiv.title: "Genlangs" and Zipf's Law: Do languages generated by ChatGPT statistically look human?
arxiv.abstract: OpenAI's GPT-4 is a Large Language Model (LLM) that can generate coherent constructed languages, or "conlangs," which we propose be called "genlangs" when generated by Artificial Intelligence (AI). arxiv.title: Enhancing Large Language Models with Climate Resources
arxiv.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have significantly transformed the landscape of artificial intelligence by demonstrating their ability in generating human-like text across diverse topics. arxiv.title: Extracting Thyroid Nodules Characteristics from Ultrasound Reports Using Transformer-based Natural Language Processing Methods
arxiv.abstract: The ultrasound characteristics of thyroid nodules guide the evaluation of thyroid cancer in patients with thyroid nodules. arxiv.title: Dense Sparse Retrieval: Using Sparse Language Models for Inference Efficient Dense Retrieval
arxiv.abstract: Vector-based retrieval systems have become a common staple for academic and industrial search applications because they provide a simple and scalable way of extending the search to leverage contextual representations for documents and queries. arxiv.title: Identifying Symptoms of Delirium from Clinical Narratives Using Natural Language Processing
arxiv.abstract: Delirium is an acute decline or fluctuation in attention, awareness, or other cognitive function that can lead to serious adverse outcomes. Despite the severe outcomes, delirium is frequently unrecognized and uncoded in patients' electronic health records (EHRs) due to its transient and diverse natu

arxiv.title: A Surface-Based Federated Chow Test Model for Integrating APOE Status, Tau Deposition Measure, and Hippocampal Surface Morphometry
arxiv.abstract: Background: Alzheimer's Disease (AD) is the most common type of age-related dementia, affecting 6.2 million people aged 65 or older according to CDC data. arxiv.title: Decoding the End-to-end Writing Trajectory in Scholarly Manuscripts
arxiv.abstract: Scholarly writing presents a complex space that generally follows a methodical procedure to plan and produce both rationally sound and creative compositions. arxiv.title: Hierarchical Vision Transformers for Cardiac Ejection Fraction Estimation
arxiv.abstract: The left ventricular of ejection fraction is one of the most important metric of cardiac function. It is used by cardiologist to identify patients who are eligible for lifeprolonging therapies. However, the assessment of ejection fraction suffers from inter-observer variability. arxiv.title: Improving extreme weather events detection with light-weight neural networks

arxiv.title: Lego-Features: Exporting modular encoder features for streaming and deliberation ASR
arxiv.abstract: In end-to-end (E2E) speech recognition models, a representational tight-coupling inevitably emerges between the encoder and the decoder.
